rationale: The cited paper is in the same substantive domain (household TV-viewing choice modeling) and could plausibly be part of the prior TV-choice foundation that the citing paper says it extends. However, the available evidence is only a single DOI-level reference match with no in-text citation context or reference text, and the modeling focus differs (intrahousehold simultaneous choice vs. sequential channel search/commercial timing). Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
